methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

data.narrative:
Archer Aviation is a publicly traded company in the Drones & Autonomous sector with a current market valuation of $5B and total capital raised exceeding $1.5B. The sector peer universe is sparse: no direct public comparables exist, and the private peer set numbers only six companies, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, Aurora Innovation, and ideaForge.

The peer-median valuation method is not applicable here due to insufficient peer density. The analysis identified only one same-stage comparable and zero adjacent-stage peers that meet narrow filtering criteria, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable triangulation. Constructing a broader peer median across the entire Drones & Autonomous sector would obscure meaningful differences in business model, stage maturity, and revenue trajectory, rendering the result unreliable for valuation support.

Given these methodological constraints, the current $5B valuation cannot be independently validated through traditional comparable-company analysis. Any valuation synthesis must acknowledge that the sector remains early-stage with limited public market pricing signals and a fragmented private peer set lacking sufficient homogeneity. Key uncertainties center on regulatory approval timelines for autonomous systems, the path to unit economics at scale, and competitive positioning relative to the five identified private peers. Additional analysis would require revenue multiples, margin profiles, or asset-based approaches, none of which are present in the available data.
